complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires direct retrieval of the foundational definition and primary purpose of functional behavioral assessment from training or textbooks without requiring application to a scenario.
construct_actually_tested: Definition and primary purpose of functional behavioral assessment in identifying behavioral antecedents, behavior patterns, and maintaining consequences

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it assesses memorized definition of "behavior function" as the reinforcement maintaining a behavior and student motivation without requiring interpretation or application to a scenario.
construct_actually_tested: Definition of behavioral function as the reinforcement or motivation maintaining a specific behavior in operant learning theory

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires interpreting a real-world scenario of classroom behavior to identify which teacher response would functionally reinforce the student behavior of calling out.
construct_actually_tested: Ability to identify functional reinforcers in classroom settings based on discriminating which teacher responses increase or maintain student behavior

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires interpreting an FBA result showing escape function and selecting an appropriate replacement behavior that serves the same function appropriately in a realistic scenario.
construct_actually_tested: Selection of functionally equivalent replacement behaviors that serve the same escape function as problem behavior in behavior intervention planning

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires analyzing a presented antecedent-behavior-consequence sequence to infer the specific behavioral function being reinforced by the consequence in the ABC relationship.
construct_actually_tested: Ability to infer behavioral function from antecedent-behavior-consequence sequences in typical classroom contexts

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires synthesizing knowledge of indirect assessment methods (interviews) and direct methods (observation) to identify complementary roles in verification and hypothesis generation.
construct_actually_tested: Integration of indirect and direct functional behavioral assessment methods to generate and verify hypotheses about behavioral functions and patterns

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires retrieving the definition of setting event and identifying an example that increases behavior likelihood from provided options without analysis or application.
construct_actually_tested: Definition and identification of setting events as antecedent conditions that increase the probability of subsequent behavior occurrence

complexity_rationale: This is an Analysis item because it requires evaluating why an extinction intervention would be ineffective when peer reinforcement continues, synthesizing multiple contingencies affecting one behavior.
construct_actually_tested: Analysis of factors preventing intervention effectiveness when multiple reinforcement sources maintain behavior beyond the targeted consequence manipulation

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires memorized knowledge of the definitional relationship between FBA as assessment and BIP as intervention planning without requiring application or comparison.
construct_actually_tested: Sequential relationship between functional behavioral assessment findings and behavior intervention plan design

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires identifying the sensory stimulation function and selecting an appropriate intervention providing legitimate sensory input rather than punishment or isolation.
construct_actually_tested: Selection of reinforcing replacement activities matched to sensory stimulation function of behavior in designing comprehensive interventions

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires interpreting a scatter plot data pattern showing contextual variation to identify that specific activities and transitions trigger the off-task behavior.
construct_actually_tested: Interpretation of scatter plot data to identify contextual and environmental factors triggering behavior occurrence in functional behavioral assessment

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires retrieving specific federal regulatory knowledge about IDEA FBA requirements related to placement changes without requiring application or interpretation.
construct_actually_tested: Specific IDEA regulations requiring functional behavioral assessment when student behavior leads to placement change decisions

complexity_rationale: This is an Analysis item because it requires evaluating how to address escape-maintained refusal when underlying fine motor difficulty contributes, synthesizing both function and skill factors.
construct_actually_tested: Integration of functional analysis findings with skill deficits in designing interventions addressing both behavioral function and underlying performance limitations

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires identifying a replacement behavior that serves the same function as the problem behavior in an appropriate manner within realistic behavioral intervention context.
construct_actually_tested: Concept of competing pathways and selection of replacement behaviors maintaining equivalence of function to problem behavior

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires determining how to respond appropriately to informal teacher observations by seeking specific operational definitions while validating input in collaborative consultation.
construct_actually_tested: Ability to balance stakeholder input with objective data collection by operationalizing vague behavioral descriptions in functional behavioral assessment

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires identifying the definitional characteristics of functional analysis (systematic manipulation of conditions to test hypotheses) from provided options.
construct_actually_tested: Definition of functional analysis as systematic experimental manipulation of conditions to test behavioral hypotheses about maintaining functions

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires interpreting a scenario where setting events (peer conflict arousal) precede behavior, selecting intervention modifying both antecedent conditions and skills.
construct_actually_tested: Application of setting event analysis to intervention design by addressing antecedent conditions and teaching coping skills for emotional dysregulation

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires understanding how specific FBA components (behavior description, frequency, contexts, functions) inform intervention design without requiring comparative analysis.
construct_actually_tested: Recognition that comprehensive functional behavioral assessment data on topography, functions, and contexts directly inform specific intervention selection

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires interpreting a behavioral scenario and determining the appropriate intervention strategy when a behavior serves multiple distinct functions, then selecting an approach that teaches a single replacement behavior addressing both functions.
construct_actually_tested: Intervention planning for behaviors serving multiple functions by selecting replacement behaviors that satisfy multiple behavioral reinforcement functions

complexity_rationale: This is an Analysis item because it requires evaluating why the replacement behavior is not increasing despite FBA-based intervention implementation, considering both implementation fidelity issues and whether replacement behavior reinforcement is weaker than problem behavior reinforcement.
construct_actually_tested: Analysis of intervention failure attributing to implementation fidelity or insufficient reinforcement strength for replacement behavior relative to problem behavior

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires retrieving foundational knowledge from school psychology that functional behavioral assessment must precede formal behavior support plan development in standard professional practice.
construct_actually_tested: Prerequisite assessment type required before developing formal behavior intervention plans for students with behavioral difficulties

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires retrieving standard behaviorist categories of primary motivations (attention, affiliation, control) as taught in school psychology without requiring application.
construct_actually_tested: Major behavioral motivations in school settings according to behaviorist theory: attention, affiliation, and control functions

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires identifying that observing antecedents and consequences in the natural classroom setting exemplifies functional behavioral assessment methodology from the definition.
construct_actually_tested: Recognition that systematic antecedent-behavior-consequence observation in natural settings constitutes functional behavioral assessment

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires retrieving standard behavioral observation documentation and analysis practices including frequency, intensity, and duration parameters from foundational school psychology knowledge.
construct_actually_tested: Standard behavioral observation parameters including frequency, intensity, and duration for documenting and analyzing target behavior

complexity_rationale: This is a Recall item because it requires sequencing the standard FBA procedural steps (define, observe, develop hypothesis, plan) from established FBA methodology without application.
construct_actually_tested: Correct sequence of functional behavioral assessment procedural steps from problem definition through hypothesis development to planning

complexity_rationale: This is an Application item because it requires interpreting Sally's FBA result showing social motivation for her behavior and then selecting environmental modification as the appropriate first intervention strategy.
construct_actually_tested: Selection of environmental modifications as appropriate initial strategy when behavior is maintained by social motivation functions
